description_long: This house features traditional Japanese architectural elements, with a multi-tiered, dark-tiled hip roof and wooden exterior walls. The structure is composed of two main volumes, one taller and set back, creating a layered silhouette. The lower level is clad in vertical wooden planks, while the upper level incorporates shoji-style windows and sliding panels. The entryway is recessed beneath a small overhanging roof, flanked by wooden fencing and stone foundation. The windows are arranged asymmetrically, with some sections featuring large, translucent panels. The overall appearance is that of a classic Japanese machiya townhouse, blending natural materials and elegant simplicity.
description: A traditional Japanese house with a dark-tiled hip roof, wooden plank walls, and shoji-style windows, featuring a recessed entryway and layered structure.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main entryway, wooden fencing, and the layered roof structure with both lower and upper stories visible.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the vertical wooden planks, stone foundation, and the upper story's shoji-style windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the long wooden wall, upper story with sliding panels, and the extended roof eaves.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the main entrance, overhanging roof, and the symmetrical arrangement of the lower and upper roof sections.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry with a small overhanging roof, wooden door, and flanking wooden fencing
